assertions.text: V1 was traveling east in the first of four lanes on a positively divided, two-way roadway.
V2 was traveling east in the same lane ahead of V1.
The front of V1 contacted the back of V2 (rear of V2 struck by front of V1).
V2 was redirected off the right side of the road.
The right side of V2 contacted the concrete traffic barrier.
Both vehicles then came to rest in the road.
V2 at final rest had a major fire.
There was at least one impact: V1's front contacting V2's rear.
Impact (V1 front to V2 rear) occurred before V2 being redirected off the right side of the road and contacting the concrete traffic barrier.

verdicts.evidence: Lane and roadway geometry (direction east, lane number, divided roadway) are not available in the vehicle telemetry channels. The available channels show speed samples such as [first_value 253.0](T2) km/h and [last_value 203.0](T2) km/h but nothing about heading or lane position, so the claim is unverifiable.

Whether V2 was ahead of V1 in the same lane and traveling east is not recorded in V1 telemetry. The speed values [first_value 253.0](T2) km/h and [last_value 203.0](T2) km/h do not indicate relative positions or headings, so this is unverifiable.

Telemetry shows a large frontal deceleration consistent with V1 striking something ahead: the crash-pulse cumulative longitudinal delta-V reached [value_at_max -89.0](T2) km/h at [t_at_max 0.14](T1) s (EDR summary [max_delta_v_long_kmh -88.55](T1) km/h), indicating a strong negative longitudinal delta-V (vehicle decelerated forward), which supports that V1's front contacted the rear of another vehicle.

Lateral displacement or trajectory of the other vehicle V2 (redirected off the right side) is not present in V1's telemetry. The crash pulse has a lateral component ([delta_v_lat final_value -22.0](T2) km/h and [max_delta_v_lat_kmh -24.15](T1) km/h) but V1 channels alone cannot confirm V2 was redirected to the right, so this is unverifiable.

Contact of V2's right side with a concrete traffic barrier is not recorded in V1 telemetry. While V1 recorded a lateral delta-V ([final_value -22.0](T2) km/h) that indicates lateral motion occurred, it does not identify V2's contact with a barrier, so the claim is unverifiable.

Final rest positions of both vehicles are not available in V1 telemetry. The crash data shows the event and delta-V ([value_at_max -89.0](T2) km/h) but does not record post-crash positions, so whether both vehicles came to rest in the road is unverifiable.
Fire in V2 at final rest is not recorded in V1 telemetry. No channel reports fire or thermal events; therefore this assertion is unverifiable.

Detectable crash-pulse indicates at least one impact: the delta-V detection lists an impact with longitudinal magnitude [magnitude_kmh 89.0](T3) km/h (signed [signed_kmh -89.0](T3)) and the EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded 1](T1), supporting that there was at least one impact (V1 contacting another vehicle ahead).

Timing within the telemetry shows the crash-pulse peak occurred at about [t_at_max 0.14](T1) s, and delta-V lateral peak at [t_at_max 0.12](T1) s; the crash-pulse indicates the primary impact (longitudinal delta-V [value_at_max -89.0](T2) km/h) happened during the recorded event. The sensors show a brake application at [t -4.5](T2) s before impact and no separate later impact events; therefore the sequence that the impact (V1 front to V2 rear) occurred before V2 contacting barrier (which would be after the initial impact and reflected in lateral delta-V) is consistent with the longitudinal impact preceding lateral motion, supporting the asserted sequence.

result.rationale: Vehicle telemetry from V1 records a single high-magnitude longitudinal crash pulse ([value_at_max -89.0](T2) km/h) and a lateral component ([final_value -22.0](T2) km/h), and detects one event ([num_events_recorded 1](T1)). These support that V1 struck something ahead, but sensors on V1 cannot confirm lane positions, other vehicle motions, final rest places, or fire, so several narrative assertions are unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
